examples.title: E-Waste Collection Algorithm
examples.description: Basic algorithm for setting up e-waste collection system
examples.code:
PROCEDURE EwasteCollection
BEGIN
 SET collection_centers = 0
 SET awareness_level = 0
 
 // Establish collection infrastructure
 FOR each municipality DO
 IF population > 10000 THEN
 CREATE collection_center
 SET collection_centers = collection_centers + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Awareness campaign
 WHILE awareness_level < target DO
 CONDUCT awareness_programs
 OFFER incentives for collection
 awareness_level = awareness_level + 10
 END WHILE
 
 // Collection process
 FOR each citizen DO
 ACCEPT e-waste at collection_point
 SORT e-waste by category
 TRANSPORT to recycling facility
 END FOR
 
 RETURN collection_rate
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the steps for establishing an e-waste collection system including infrastructure setup, awareness campaigns, and collection logistics. It helps understand the operational aspects of e-waste management.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n*m) - dependent on population and infrastructure

examples.title: Material Recovery Process
examples.description: Step-by-step process for extracting materials from e-waste
examples.code:
PROCEDURE MaterialRecovery(e_waste)
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Manual Sorting
 SEPARATE e_waste INTO categories:
 - Temperature exchange equipment
 - Screens and monitors
 - Small IT equipment
 - Batteries
 - Printed circuit boards (PCBs)
 
 // Step 2: Dismantling
 FOR each item IN category DO
 REMOVE hazardous components
 SEPARATE reusable parts
 END FOR
 
 // Step 3: Size Reduction
 SHRED all components INTO small pieces
 SEPARATE magnetic materials (iron)
 SEPARATE non-ferrous metals (aluminum, copper)
 
 // Step 4: Advanced Separation
 FOR each fraction DO
 APPLY eddy current separation
 APPLY density separation
 END FOR
 
 // Step 5: Refining
 PROCESS PCBs USING:
 - Pyrometallurgy (smelting)
 - OR Hydrometallurgy (chemical leaching)
 
 // Step 6: Output
 RETURN recovered_materials:
 - Precious metals (gold, silver)
 - Base metals (copper, aluminum)
 - Plastics
 - Glass
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows the material recovery process from e-waste, progressing from manual sorting through to advanced separation techniques for extracting valuable materials.

examples.title: EPR Compliance Checker
examples.description: Algorithm to verify producer compliance with EPR obligations
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CheckEPRCompliance(producer)
BEGIN
 SET collected = 0
 SET target = producer.sales * 0.3 // 30% collection target
 
 // Calculate collection achievement
 FOR each quarter DO
 collected = collected + producer.ewaste_collected
 END FOR
 
 // Check collection target
 IF collected >= target THEN
 PRINT "Collection target MET"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Collection target NOT MET"
 DEFICIT = target - collected
 CHARGE producer.financial_obligation = DEFICIT * recycling_cost
 END IF
 
 // Verify recycling authorization
 FOR each recycler IN producer.authorized_recyclers DO
 IF recycler.is_registered AND recycler.has_permit THEN
 PRINT "Recycler authorized"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Unauthorized recycler - violation"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check reporting compliance
 IF producer.submitted_annual_report THEN
 VERIFY report.accuracy
 IF report.verified THEN
 PRINT "Compliant"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Report discrepancy - penalty"
 END IF
 ELSE
 PRINT "Missing report - violation"
 END IF
 
 RETURN compliance_status
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm verifies if electronics producers are meeting their Extended Producer Responsibility obligations including collection targets, authorized recycler usage, and reporting requirements.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - dependent on number of recyclers and reports

examples.title: E-Waste Impact Assessment
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate environmental impact score for e-waste disposal methods
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates an environmental impact score for different e-waste disposal methods based on various pollution factors, helping decision-makers choose the most sustainable option.

examples.title: E-Waste Classification System
examples.description: Algorithm to classify e-waste according to WEEE categories
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ClassifyEwaste(item)
BEGIN
 INPUT item.description
 
 // WEEE Category Classification
 SELECT CASE item.type
 CASE "refrigerator", "air_conditioner", "heat_pump"
 category = "Temperature Exchange Equipment"
 code = "Cat1"
 
 CASE "tv_monitor", "laptop_screen", "lcd_display"
 category = "Screens and Monitors"
 code = "Cat2"
 
 CASE "fluorescent_lamp", "led_lamp", "bulb"
 category = "Lamps"
 code = "Cat3"
 
 CASE "washing_machine", "dryer", "dishwasher"
 category = "Large Equipment"
 code = "Cat4"
 
 CASE "vacuum_cleaner", "toaster", "microwave"
 category = "Small Equipment"
 code = "Cat5"
 
 CASE "mobile_phone", "router", "printer", "keyboard"
 category = "Small IT and Telecom Equipment"
 code = "Cat6"
 
 CASE DEFAULT
 category = "Uncategorized"
 code = "Cat0"
 END SELECT
 
 // Check hazardous content
 IF item.contains_mercury OR item.contains_lead OR 
 item.contains_cadmium OR item.contains_bromine THEN
 hazardous_status = "HAZARDOUS"
 ELSE
 hazardous_status = "NON_HAZARDOUS"
 END IF
 
 RETURN {category: category, code: code, hazardous: hazardous_status}
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm classifies e-waste items according to the six WEEE categories and identifies hazardous content, essential for proper routing to appropriate recycling processes.
